gene_region.label: LDLR transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical LDLR genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: LDLR encodes the low-density lipoprotein receptor, which binds apoB/apoE-containing lipoproteins and mediates LDL uptake through clathrin-associated endocytosis.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local LDLR bundle is familial-hypercholesterolemia and lipid-uptake oriented. High-impact LDLR findings require clinical-grade classification, inheritance, phenotype, and lipid data.
variant_effect_overview: LDLR variants can affect ligand binding, trafficking, recycling, splicing, or receptor abundance.
Common markers are risk modifiers, while pathogenic LDLR variants can have monogenic familial hypercholesterolemia implications.
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: LDLR methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a LDL particle binding, receptor-mediated endocytosis, cholesterol uptake, and familial hypercholesterolemia biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for LDLR.
Because LDLR biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against LDL particle binding, receptor-mediated endocytosis, cholesterol uptake, and familial hypercholesterolemia biology.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use LDLR promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for LDL particle binding, receptor-mediated endocytosis, cholesterol uptake, and familial hypercholesterolemia biology.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000130164: LDLR coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: LDLR biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: LDL particle binding, receptor-mediated endocytosis, cholesterol uptake, and familial hypercholesterolemia biology
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an LDLR receptor-mediated-endocytosis thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to LDL particle clearance, LDL-C levels, apoB lipoprotein uptake, familial hypercholesterolemia review, or atherosclerotic lipid biology.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs688 is curated as a LDLR marker for Familial hypercholesterolemia review context, LDL-C and apoB lipoprotein uptake. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the LDL particle binding, receptor-mediated endocytosis, cholesterol uptake, and familial hypercholesterolemia biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag LDL particle binding, receptor-mediated endocytosis, cholesterol uptake, and familial hypercholesterolemia biology.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
research_context: Use this marker as a LDLR pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair rs688 with LDLR methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.

literature_findings.genotypes: rs688 or linked LDLR locus genotype states
literature_findings.phenotype: Familial hypercholesterolemia review context, LDL-C and apoB lipoprotein uptake
literature_findings.finding: The local curation treats rs688 as a gene-specific marker for Familial hypercholesterolemia review context, LDL-C and apoB lipoprotein uptake and LDL particle binding, receptor-mediated endocytosis, cholesterol uptake, and familial hypercholesterolemia biology.
